message:
chore(beads): close ft-7bcox after FrameWriter Drop catch_unwind landed

Transitions ft-7bcox in_progress → closed with a detailed
close_reason describing the shipped fix. No other beads are
touched.

Bead: ft-7bcox — [LOW] FrameWriter Drop flush may abort on
double-panic during unwind (7c8deb1d smell).

Status transition:
- in_progress → closed
- updated_at: 2026-04-26T01:54:43Z → 2026-04-26T02:00:38Z
- closed_at: 2026-04-26T02:00:38Z (set)
- close_reason added (verbatim, written by a parallel agent
  after rch verification on /tmp/ft-cc6-target):
    "2bb39b2d (linter-committed from my edits) wraps
     FrameWriter::drop's flush call in std::panic::catch_unwind
     with AssertUnwindSafe so a flush panic during a panic-unwind
     doesn't trip Rust's panic-during-unwind abort rule.
     AssertUnwindSafe is sound: closure Result discarded,
     &mut self never observed again, buffer/writer drop after
     closure regardless. Two new regression tests at
     recording.rs:1245 + 1301:
       (1) frame_writer_drop_completes_during_panic_unwind_without_aborting
           — integration test panicking inside a closure that
           owns a live FrameWriter with buffered frames; passing
           assertions prove Drop did not abort, and the buffered
           frame's bytes still made it to disk so the happy path
           runs;
       (2) frame_writer_drop_catch_unwind_swallows_inner_panic
           — standalone fence on the catch_unwind +
           AssertUnwindSafe idiom against future refactors.
     5 frame_writer tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target."

Cross-references:
- The fix landed earlier on this branch as commit
  2bb39b2d ("fix(recording): wrap FrameWriter::drop flush in
  catch_unwind to avoid double-panic abort") which also
  contains the two regression tests called out in the
  close_reason. The companion bead-state sync that flipped
  ft-7bcox open → in_progress was committed as 7ef38163; this
  commit completes the lifecycle by flipping in_progress →
  closed now that rch verification has passed.

Verified before commit:
- Only ft-7bcox is mutated in the diff (1 insertion, 1
  deletion).
- The transition is in_progress → closed; no reopen, no
  status downgrade, no destructive deletion.

Co-author note: bead state mutation was emitted by another
agent in the same multi-agent session; committed verbatim per
AGENTS.md's "treat parallel-agent changes as your own" rule.

 {"id":"ft-76d9i","title":"[audit] storage/search: validate_fts_query doubles full-scan cost on lexical queries","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:33:26.455763Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:55:02.015811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:55:02.015533Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 2099121d: validate_fts_query now uses SELECT 1 ... LIMIT 1 (short-circuits on first matching rowid) instead of SELECT COUNT(*) ... LIMIT 1 (LIMIT does not cap aggregate work — full match-set scan ran twice per lexical search). Empty-match-set semantics preserved via QueryReturnedNoRows → Ok promotion. 2 new regression tests pin empty-set validation and 4-shape happy path (simple, prefix*, AND, OR). All 14 existing fts_search_* tests pass against /tmp/ft-pane0-76d9i-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3610,"issue_id":"ft-76d9i","author":"jemanuel","text":"Perf/contention finding from 2026-04-23 storage audit: every lexical FTS search currently does a redundant preflight full scan before the real query. Evidence: validate_fts_query runs SELECT COUNT(*) FROM output_segments_fts WHERE output_segments_fts MATCH ?1 LIMIT 1 at storage.rs:15010-15016, and search_fts_with_snippets calls it unconditionally before preparing/executing the actual snippet/BM25 query at storage.rs:15042-15048. COUNT(*) with LIMIT 1 still has to enumerate all matches for broad FTS terms, so common queries pay for two MATCH walks and hold the read connection longer under load. Expected: syntax validation should be a cheap prepare/step of the real query shape or a SELECT rowid ... LIMIT 1 probe, not an aggregate count over the whole match set.","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:34:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-7awje","title":"Align robot_types envelope serialization with CLI omit-null contract","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:33:35.810723Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:55.614794Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:41:55.614462Z","close_reason":"Already landed concurrently in 8fdfd114 (robot_types omit-null envelope serialization + regression tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7axpo","title":"protocol_recovery deterministic jitter defeats thundering-herd protection","description":"# [HIGH] protocol_recovery: fake jitter

**Subsystem:** protocol_recovery / retry
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/protocol_recovery.rs (`RecoveryConfig::delay_for_attempt`)

## The hole

\`\`\`rust
let jitter_seed = ((attempt as f64 * 7.13).sin().abs()).mul_add(2.0, -1.0);
let jittered_ms = jitter_range.mul_add(jitter_seed, capped_ms).max(1.0);
\`\`\`

`sin(attempt * 7.13)` is fully deterministic. Every RecoveryEngine in the
process produces the **same** delay for a given attempt number. In a large
agent swarm, if 100 panes lose their mux connection simultaneously (mux
server restart), every engine schedules identical retry timings: attempt=0
→ delay_X, attempt=1 → delay_Y, etc. They all pile in at the same
instants — the exact thundering-herd collision that jitter exists to
prevent.

Pre-fix: `jitter_fraction: 0.1` looked like 10% decorrelation but produced
zero decorrelation across engines.

## Fix

Replaced the deterministic `sin()`-derived seed with
`rand::rng().random_range(-jitter_range..=jitter_range)` — same approach
already used in `retry.rs::RetryPolicy::delay_for_attempt`. Consistent with
how the other module in the same crate spells jitter.

Kept the `jitter_range > 0.0` fast-path so `jitter_fraction == 0.0` is
still bit-exact deterministic (tests rely on this).

## Caller impact

`RecoveryEngine::execute` uses `delay_for_attempt` to sleep between
protocol-error retries. No test depends on deterministic jitter
(`delay_for_attempt_*` tests set `jitter_fraction: 0.0`), so the fix is
drop-in.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:26.938808Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:43:28.102548Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:43:28.102198Z","close_reason":"Fix already shipped in fdd1e817; regression tests added in bf4a63b9 (delay_for_attempt_jitter_is_randomized_across_calls + delay_for_attempt_zero_jitter_is_deterministic).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["protocol-recovery","reliability","retry"]}
-{"id":"ft-7bcox","title":"[LOW] FrameWriter Drop flush may abort on double-panic during unwind (7c8deb1d smell)","description":"Fresh-eyes review of 7c8deb1d: Drop impl does I/O via BufWriter::flush. If flush panics during panic unwinding (OOM, broken-pipe custom impl), Rust aborts the process (double-panic). std File doesn't panic on I/O errors so low real-world risk, but defensive catch_unwind inside drop would harden.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:18.017407Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:54:43.234734Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["recording","review","review-of-review"]}

If flush panics during panic unwinding (OOM, broken-pipe custom impl), Rust aborts the process (double-panic). std File doesn't panic on I/O errors so low real-world risk, but defensive catch_unwind inside drop would harden.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:18.017407Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:00:38.491092Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T02:00:38.490826Z","close_reason":"2bb39b2d (linter-committed from my edits) wraps FrameWriter::drop's flush call in std::panic::catch_unwind with AssertUnwindSafe so a flush panic during a panic-unwind doesn't trip Rust's panic-during-unwind abort rule. AssertUnwindSafe is sound: closure Result discarded, &mut self never observed again, buffer/writer drop after closure regardless. Two new regression tests at recording.rs:1245 + 1301: (1) frame_writer_drop_completes_during_panic_unwind_without_aborting — integration test panicking inside a closure that owns a live FrameWriter with buffered frames; passing assertions prove Drop did not abort, and the buffered frame's bytes still made it to disk so the happy path runs; (2) frame_writer_drop_catch_unwind_swallows_inner_panic — standalone fence on the catch_unwind + AssertUnwindSafe idiom against future refactors. 5 frame_writer tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["recording","review","review-of-review"]}
 {"id":"ft-7cp47","title":"frankenterm-core full-feature lib build fails in vendored mux client and web framework","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane22","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:43.079602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:26.866164Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:26.865405Z","close_reason":"Verified fixed: cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes in 2m49s (0 errors, 231 warnings only), and cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --all-features also passes in 2m39s (0 errors). The vendored mux client + web_framework build blockers referenced in the bead title are resolved; most likely by the fdd1e817 + 9e1bd63b ft-xbnl0.2.5 cfg-gate stripping sweep that collapsed the dual-runtime seam. No pending vendored-mux-client or web_framework compile errors remain under any feature combination we can test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7do6c","title":"storage: deferred FTS sync skips seq=0 segment forever (seq > last_indexed_seq off-by-one)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15902-15950 get_unindexed_segments_sync uses WHERE pane_id = ?1 AND seq > ?2 (strict >). crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:12355 append_segment_sync assigns next_seq = COALESCE(MAX(seq) + 1, 0), so the VERY FIRST segment in a fresh pane gets seq=0. Meanwhile sync_fts_for_pane at line 15966 initializes max_seq from progress.last_indexed_seq OR defaults to 0 when no progress exists: 'let last_seq = progress.as_ref().map_or(0, |p| p.last_indexed_seq);'. First-ever sync of a fresh pane hits 'WHERE seq > 0' which excludes the seq=0 segment — the pane's first captured output is silently dropped from the FTS index and never recoverable via sync_fts catchup. This is the defect the ft-wk5fo close-note explicitly surfaced but did not fix ('tests use seq 1..=N, separate bead for that fix'). Scope: a fresh pane's first segment is the command prompt / shell startup / first workflow output — high-value searchable content. Users get empty search results for anything only in that segment. Fix: distinguish 'never indexed' from 'indexed up through seq=0'. Cleanest minimal fix: in sync_fts_for_pane, when progress is None AND total_indexed == 0, query with an inclusive WHERE pane_id = ?1 (no seq filter) for the first batch. After the first batch writes progress, subsequent batches use the strict > query as today. Regression test must construct a pane whose first segment has seq=0 and assert sync_fts_for_pane indexes it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:14:14.846835Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:33.697334Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:33.697066Z","close_reason":"Landed in d37376b0 (fix(storage): unbreak sync_fts_for_pane call site + index seq=0 on first sync). Call-site updated to pass include_from_zero=true on the very first batch of a never-synced pane, false otherwise. had_prior_progress tracks the 'never indexed' sentinel. Regression test ft_7do6c_catchup_indexes_seq_zero_on_first_sync pins seq=0 indexing + resume invariant. Also unbroke main — 49937d56 had landed my half-done fix (signature + docstring only, no call-site update), causing an E0061 compile error at storage.rs:16004. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
